subject: Smart Cities

tenders.title: Czechia – Information systems – Mzdový, personální a docházkový informační systém
tenders.bid_fit_summary: This opportunity involves an information system for payroll, personnel, and attendance, which does not align with PARTTEAM's core products of digital signage displays and self-service kiosks. The CPV codes suggest a focus on software solutions rather than hardware, which is outside PARTTEAM's expertise.
tenders.strategic_link: This opportunity does not align with any known strategic objectives of PARTTEAM, as it focuses on software systems rather than hardware solutions.
tenders.recommended_action: PARTTEAM should not pursue this opportunity as it does not align with their product offerings or strategic objectives.

tenders.title: Germany – Electrical wiring and fitting work – Umbau und Erweiterung Medico Palais Gebäudeautomationsanlagen
tenders.bid_fit_summary: The opportunity involves electrical wiring and fitting work for building automation systems in Germany, which aligns with PARTTEAM's capabilities in smart city solutions. However, the focus on electrical work may not fully align with our core expertise in digital signage and kiosks.
tenders.strategic_link: This opportunity could support PARTTEAM's objective to expand its presence in the smart cities sector, particularly in the European market.
tenders.recommended_action: Monitor the opportunity for further details and potential partnerships that align more closely with our product offerings.

tenders.title: Poland – Software package and information systems – Zakup systemu ERP kadrowo-płacowo-ewidencyjnego

The product fit is weak as the company does not specialize in software solutions of this nature.
tenders.strategic_link: There is no direct strategic objective alignment as PARTTEAM focuses on hardware solutions for digital signage and kiosks, not ERP software.
tenders.recommended_action: PARTTEAM should not pursue this opportunity as it falls outside its core business area.

private_opportunities.title: DHS, CBP award $4.5B in new contracts under OBBB for Smart Wall ...
private_opportunities.snippet:
San Diego 1 Project – Awarded to BCCG Joint Venture for $483,486,600 for the construction of approximately nine miles of new Smart Wall and approximately 52 miles of system attributes in USBP’s San Diego Sector in California.
 El Centro 1 Project – Awarded to Fisher Sand & Gravel Co. for $574,000,000 for the construction of approximately eight miles of new primary Smart Wall and the installation of approximately 63 miles of system attributes in USBP’s El Centro and San Diego Sectors in California.
 Yuma 1 Project – Awarded to Barnard Spencer Joint Venture for $199,508,100 for the construction of approximately 60 miles of system attributes in USBP’s Yuma Sector in Arizona and California. [...] El Paso 3 Project – Awarded to BCCG Joint Venture for $850,361,073 for the construction of approximately 42 miles of new primary Smart Wall, approximately 6 miles of new secondary border wall and completion of approximately 46 miles of system attributes in USBP’s El Paso Sector in Texas.
 Del Rio 1 Project – Awarded to BCCG Joint Venture for $565,047,300 for the construction of approximately 22 miles of new primary Smart Wall (which includes replacing approximately two miles of old barrier) and deployment of approximately 40 miles of waterborne barrier system in USBP’s Del Rio Sector, in the Eagle Pass Area of Responsibility, in Texas. [...] To cut through bureaucratic red tape and expedite the construction of the Smart Wall, Secretary Noem also issued two new waivers for approximately nine miles of Smart Wall in the Border Patrol’s San Diego Sector and approximately 30 miles of new Smart Wall located in New Mexico within the El Paso Sector.

The first 10 contracts funded by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (and remaining FY 2021 wall appropriations) awarded in September include:
private_opportunities.bid_fit_summary: This opportunity involves the construction of Smart Walls, which is not directly aligned with PARTTEAM's expertise in digital signage displays and self-service kiosks. The project focuses on infrastructure development rather than digital communication or interactive solutions.
private_opportunities.strategic_link: This opportunity does not directly serve any known strategic objectives of PARTTEAM, as it is outside the company's core competencies in digital signage and kiosks.
private_opportunities.recommended_action: PARTTEAM should not pursue this opportunity as it does not align with their product offerings or strategic goals.

private_opportunities.title: IDC's 2026 Smart Cities and Communities North America Awards
private_opportunities.snippet:
For more details contact Ann Whiting / awhiting@idc.com

  Nominate now
 ### The Nomination Process

  It is free to apply, and the online application takes 15–20 minutes to complete. Anyone can nominate a project, but only the public sector organization that implemented the project is eligible to win the award.

  Deadline to complete is January 15, 2026.

  We are looking for:

  + Implemented projects (not ideas or planned initiatives) that leverage emerging technologies— such as AI, GenAI, edge computing, cloud—to solve real challenges.
  + Initiatives that improve livability, resiliency, and economic opportunity for communities while fostering accessible, user friendly experiences and services. [...] Smart cities and communities are state and local governments at the forefront of technology innovation and adoption.  Working across departments and with the public, smart cities are the catalyst for urban transformation that creates new services, intelligent operations, and community resiliency. [...] Sustainable Infrastructure (e.g. Smart Buildings, Smart Water, Smart Lighting, Waste Collection, Environmental Monitoring, Resiliency)
 ### 9.

  Transportation (e.g. Connected, Autonomous, Electric and Shared Vehicles, Public Transit, Parking, Transit Hubs, Traffic Management, Transportation Infrastructure)
 ### 10.

  Urban Planning and Land Use (e.g. Permitting, Licensing, Inspection & Zoning; Digital Twins, Community Resiliency)

## Join us for the 9th year of the IDC Smart Cities and Communities Awards program!

Nominate now

## IDC Environmental Policy
private_opportunities.bid_fit_summary: This opportunity involves nominating a project for the IDC's 2026 Smart Cities and Communities North America Awards. PARTTEAM & OEMKIOSKS could potentially nominate a project involving their digital signage or self-service kiosks, but the award is primarily for public sector organizations.
private_opportunities.strategic_link: There is no direct strategic objective alignment available from the knowledge base for this opportunity.
private_opportunities.recommended_action: Monitor the opportunity for potential partnerships with public sector organizations that could utilize PARTTEAM's products.

executive_takeaway: The biggest opportunity this week is the German tender for electrical wiring and fitting work related to building automation systems. While it does not perfectly align with our core expertise, it presents a chance to expand our presence in the smart cities sector in Europe. Leadership should prioritize monitoring this opportunity for further details and explore potential partnerships that could leverage our digital signage and kiosk solutions. Additionally, the IDC Smart Cities Awards could be a strategic avenue to increase visibility by partnering with public sector organizations.

── EXECUTIVE TAKEAWAY ───────────────────────────────
The biggest opportunity this week is the German tender for electrical wiring and fitting work related to building automation systems. While it does not perfectly align with our core expertise, it presents a chance to expand our presence in the smart cities sector in Europe. Leadership should prioritize monitoring this opportunity for further details and explore potential partnerships that could leverage our digital signage and kiosk solutions. Additionally, the IDC Smart Cities Awards could be a strategic avenue to increase visibility by partnering with public sector organizations.
